posts.name: Pritee - Most people think service growth comes from hiring more staff.
👀 We spoke to Asif from Safy's Chemist. - Carousel
posts._action: date update — Asif carousel was stuck at Jan 7

posts.name: TV_FragmentationHook_v2
posts.caption:
Count how many apps you open before you finish one consultation.

EPS.
Consultation template.
Booking system.
Patient record.
Messaging tool.
Maybe a spreadsheet.

That is five systems for one 15-minute consultation.

Every handoff is a risk.
Every tab switch costs time.
Every manual note is a chance for something to go wrong.

Titanverse puts the whole flow in one place.

Consultation. Prescribing. Records. Services.

No more tabs.
No more double entry.
No more gaps.

One app. One flow.

#Titanverse #PharmacyWorkflow #PharmacyServices
posts.idea: Updated fragmentation hook v2. The original Oct/Nov 2025 fragmentation posts were the highest-performing Titanverse content. This is a fresh angle: 'count how many apps you open.' Short, punchy single image. Dark purple background, bold white text showing the count of 5 systems. Titanverse logo. No UI screenshots — pure copy-led.

posts.name: TITAN_Kanav_CaseStudy
posts.idea: Case study from Kanav interview (May 2025 cohort). Read _interviews-processed/Kanav.md first before writing this. Extract the strongest tipping point + before/after numbers. Single image format — photo of Kanav at his pharmacy or branded dark background with key stat. Hook to be written from the brief.

posts.name: TV_BroadwayPharmacy_MichaelBall_Spotlight
posts.caption:
An honest review from a Director who just spent the afternoon with us.

"A platform built with operators in mind, not just designed in isolation."

That is from Michael Ball, Director at Cure Clinics, after hands-on time with Titanverse at Broadway Pharmacy.

He did not reach out to us.
He posted it publicly.

What stood out was not the feature list.

It was the thinking behind it.

A platform built for how pharmacy actually works.
Not designed in isolation.

That is the only kind of feedback that matters.

#Titanverse #PharmacyServices #CommunityPharmacy
posts.idea: Customer spotlight: Michael Ball, Director at Cure Clinics. Unsolicited public post about hands-on time with Titanverse at Broadway Pharmacy. MUST get permission from Michael via DM before scheduling. Single image: dark Titanverse background with his quote in large white bold text. His name and title below. Broadway Pharmacy / Cure Clinics credit at bottom. Titanverse logo bottom right. Only schedule this if Michael gives permission.
posts._action: new post — requires Michael Ball permission before publishing

posts.name: TV_PharmAppy_Integration_May26
posts.caption:
Pharmacy websites should not be separate from pharmacy services.

When a patient books through PharmAppy, that consultation needs to live somewhere.

Not in a separate system.
Not manually transferred.

With Titanverse, it does.

The booking.
The consultation.
The record.

Connected. From first click to final note.

Priory Pharmacy is already live with this.
Broadway is next.

#Titanverse #PharmAppy #PharmacyServices
posts.idea: PharmAppy + Titanverse integration update. Priory Pharmacy live, Broadway next. Two logos connected by a line. Clean, minimal dark background. This is a soft product announcement post — the booking → consultation → record flow in one connected system. No UI screenshots needed.

Write from whatever specific quote or outcome emerges from the brief.

posts.name: TITAN_158M_Clawback_Carousel
posts.caption:
£158 million.

That is what pharmacies left on the table last year.

Not through fraud.
Not through negligence.

Through under-claiming.

The difference between pharmacies that lost money and those that didn't is not luck.

Swipe to see what they did differently.

💬 Has your pharmacy been hit by a clawback? Drop your experience in the comments.

#PharmacyFirst #NHS #CommunityPharmacy #TitanPMR
posts.idea: The £158 million clawback carousel. Originally scheduled Jan 29 — never posted. Policy/finance angle. NHS reclaims underclaimed money from pharmacies. High-stakes education carousel. Structure: Hook (the £158m number) → What happened → Why it matters → What pharmacies at risk have in common → How to protect your income → How Titan tracks this → CTA (comment/engagement). Design: dark background, large £158m in white, each slide one step in the explanation.
posts._action: new post — replaces the stale Jan 29 draft
